title: Oversight model: AgDR-after-research instead of prompts, PR as the hard gate, optional Spec→Plan gate, planner-chosen process weight
context: v1 hard-gated every human phase, idling the fleet on one reviewer's latency — contradicting Colin's prior rejection of pause gates in favor of async review (AgDR system, June 10-11 2026; PHI-028).

options.name: hard-gates-everywhere
options.rationale: Every human-gated phase blocks until stamped; zero unreviewed compounding.
options.tradeoff: Fleet idles on human latency; one person becomes the bottleneck the mission says to avoid.

options.name: async-everywhere
options.rationale: Nothing waits; all review is post-hoc ratification.
options.tradeoff: Wrong architecture calls execute downstream phases before any human sees them.

options.name: pr-gate-plus-optional-spec-gate
options.rationale: In-flight, agents research then write AgDRs (steelman + blast radius, per PHI-028) instead of prompting; hard-escalation domains (security, prod deploys, secrets, frozen contracts) still halt. Every plan ends at a PR whose body is the rich review profile; merge is binding ratification. An optional Spec→Plan gate blocks pre-seeding where decisions compound. The planner picks process weight (full/standard/patch) and justifies it in the SDR.
options.tradeoff: Unratified-but-executed work can compound within a phase before review; revert cost bounded by phase branches but not zero. Planner may under-weight process to dodge overhead.

reasoning: Aligns Switchboard with the proven cross-project AgDR model: decide-and-proceed with steelman records, async-but-binding review at the PR boundary (PHI-028's first validation overturned 2 of 15 AgDRs and surfaced a 3.3x cost defect before lock-in). Two invariants are not plannable: every plan ends at a PR gate, and every task passes independent verification (PHI-030). AgDR verdict history at merge gates accumulates the earned-autonomy track record for free.
